one_line_ask: Render our authored InDesign parts-catalog template against the master parts CSV into a proof, then audit it page by page — read back every printed spec row, cross-check it against the source data, and hand back a flagged-pages proof extract plus a findings report calling out overset/clipped part numbers, blank spec cells, and section-count mismatches before we send ~100 pages to print.
brief: Ironclad Elevator & Electrical Supply is a wholesale distributor of electrical and elevator replacement parts selling to service technicians, maintenance supervisors, and electrical contractors. Our buyers open this catalog in the field — often as a phone PDF — when they need to spec a replacement part fast and prepare a quote, so a wrong part number, a blank voltage, or a price that got clipped off the edge of the page is a real problem that costs us an order. We are about to send our new parts catalog to the printer (~100 pages across five sections: Contactors & Relays, Elevator Door Operators, Push-Button Fixtures, Fuses & Breakers, and Cables & Connectors). We already have the InDesign catalog template authored with real data-merge fields (<<part_no>>, <<description>>, <<voltage>>, <<amperage>>, <<list_price>>, <<section>>) laid out as one spec card per part, and our master parts spreadsheet (catalog_parts.csv, ~180 SKUs). Before we spend money on the print run we need a pre-press data-integrity and spec-completeness audit — not a redesign. Specifically: (1) run our authored template through the data merge against the CSV and export a proof PDF so we can see exactly what will p
